title: Request to CIA for 2016 budget for Office of Public Affairs

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

The 2016 annual budget for the Office of Public Affairs of the Central Intelligence Agency.

I am the Director of Research Operations at a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization called The Decoding Culture Foundation as well as the editor-in-chief and a contributor to a research magazine published by said foundation titled "Cultural Engineering Studies." These documents will be used for public education and research purposes. I therefore request a waiver of all fees that may otherwise apply. Attached is a document with organization letterhead confirming my position, as well as the organization's 501(c)(3) application and approval form.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Brett Carollo

title: Navy support for 2026 film "Project Hail Mary"

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Navy support for 2026 film "Project Hail Mary"
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

All documents pertaining to collaboration between the US Navy and the production of the 2026 film "Project Hail Mary," starring Ryan Gosling. Such documents may include but should not be limited to: script notes; production assistance agreements; photographs; and all records of communications between employees of the Navy and members of the cast, crew, and production company of "Project Hail Mary," including memos, notes of meetings and conference calls, emails, and other forms of communication.

"Project Hail Mary" (under the title "Hail Mary") appears in a list of Navy-supported productions for the year 2024 that was released to me through FOIA. That list is attached to this request.

I am the Director of Research Operations at a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization called The Decoding Culture Foundation as well as the editor-in-chief and a contributor to a research magazine published by said foundation titled "Cultural Engineering Studies." These documents will be used for public education and research purposes. I therefore request a waiver of all fees that may otherwise apply. Attached is a document with organization letterhead confirming my position, as well as the organization's 501(c)(3) application and approval form.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Brett Carollo

title: Navy support for Mission: Impossible - The Final Reckoning

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Navy support for Mission: Impossible - The Final Reckoning
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

All documents pertaining to collaboration between the US Navy and the production of the 2025 feature film "Mission: Impossible - The Final Reckoning," directed by Christopher McQuarrie. Such documents may include but should not be not limited to: script notes; production assistance agreements; photographs; and all records of communications between employees of the Navy and members of the cast, crew, and production company of "Mission: Impossible - The Final Reckoning," including memos, notes of meetings and conference calls, emails, and other forms of communication.

I previously submitted an identical request, and received a response of "no responsive documents"; however, this film appears (under the title "Mission Impossible 8") in a list of Navy-supported productions for the year 2024 that has since been released to me through FOIA. It is therefore reasonable to conclude that the previous search was not thorough and that documents do in fact exist pertaining to this collaboration.

I am the Director of Research Operations at a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization called The Decoding Culture Foundation as well as the editor-in-chief and a contributor to a research magazine published by said foundation titled "Cultural Engineering Studies." These documents will be used for public education and research purposes. I therefore request a waiver of all fees that may otherwise apply. Attached is a document with organization letterhead confirming my position, as well as the organization's 501(c)(3) application and approval form.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Brett Carollo
